author: Sadok Merkikou
msg:
Good morning Unity family.

I had an idea, and perhaps you could share it with the team.
You know, like me, that people love the word "free."
But if I had to tell each of my ULOs that they have to buy credits for their lease at a later date, they'd start complaining again.
Isn't it a good idea to let people earn money and then have them pay for the credits based on what they've earned?
People see that as free money, and yet they'll pay for it without a problem.
Perhaps it's also an idea to change something in the application.
A pop-up for the UNO before issuing a license.
A pop-up asking whether they or the ULO will pay for the credits.
If these are transferred to the ULO, a question will pop up asking, "Pay for your credits first to use your license." Of course, this is all for a later date, when people have to buy the credits to activate their licenses, but it's better to think about it now than later.

I'll hear from people if they think it's a good idea or not.

Thanks for your feedback.

author: Dodo | MRCY Nodes | UNITY - MN - WM - CHIRP
msg: Network telemetry is the automated collection, measurement, and export of real-time performance data (metadata and traffic) from network devices like routers and switches to an external system for in-depth analysis, providing deep visibility into traffic patterns, bottlenecks, security threats, and overall network health. It uses high-speed protocols (like gRPC, UDP) and modern data models (like YANG) to offer much richer, faster insights than older methods, enabling proactive management and rapid incident response, crucial for modern, complex networks.

author: Sophie | I will never DM you first
msg:
We want to clarify some language around Unity Nodes and earnings. From a legal and regulatory standpoint, nothing related to Unity Nodes should be described as “passive.” Participation always requires intentional action. This includes installing and enabling modules, accepting and maintaining licenses, monitoring performance, and sharing rewards appropriately. These steps matter, and they are part of active participation in the network.

That said, we also understand the practical distinction people are trying to make. Some modules are less time-intensive than others and can run in the background once properly configured, while other tasks require more regular involvement. The key point is that while effort levels vary, all participation involves setup, consent, oversight, and responsibility. We want to be precise, transparent, and aligned with both reality and regulatory expectations.

author: Manuel Orozco
msg:
Discrepancies in Profit Figures: Correspondence with Support.
Overview of Profit Figure Inconsistencies.
I have reviewed some of the comments regarding profit calculations, and after careful consideration, I believe they are not entirely accurate. Specifically, the figures reported by the ULOS system differ markedly from those shown by the OPERATOR NODE (ONE), with ULOS reflecting significantly lower profits.
Contacting Support for Clarification
Due to these inconsistencies, I reached out to the support team on 01/01/2026 to seek clarification. Their response, received on 03/01/2026, is detailed below.
Support Team Response
Hi Manuel Orozco,
"Thank you for taking the time to share this detailed feedback.
We understand your concerns, and you're correct that we are currently in a testing phase. The figures shown in UNO and ULO do not yet reflect final or definitive earnings, and discrepancies like the ones you described are being reviewed.
Since you've already submitted a report, it has been acknowledged and is with the team as part of the items under review. We appreciate your patience while these calculations and displays continue to be refined.
Thank you again for your detailed input — it’s very helpful during this stage.
Kind regards,
Unity Nodes Support Team"
Current Status
On 13/01/2026, I received another message stating that my ticket had been resolved. However, in reality, the issue remains unchanged and the discrepancies persist.
